req.title: Upload onboarding document
req.statement: An employee shall upload onboarding documents up to 10MB in PDF, PNG, or JPG format; files outside this must be rejected.
preconditions: authenticated

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines three distinct equivalence classes for file uploads: (1) valid files <=10MB in PDF/PNG/JPG, (2) files exceeding 10MB (HTTP 413), and (3) files with disallowed MIME types (HTTP 415). Boundary Value Analysis drives tests at exactly 10MB and 10MB+1 byte. A Decision Table covers the cross-product of size x mime_type for error pathways. State Transition Coverage validates the post-upload state change (document stored, virus scan queued). Negative Path tests confirm proper rejection messaging. The test suite uses pytest with a shared authenticated session fixture and a file-factory helper to keep each test self-contained and idempotent.

tests.name: test_upload_valid_pdf_under_limit_stores_document_and_queues_scan
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
Target endpoint /documents/upload is reachable
A well-formed PDF file of 5MB is available
actions: Generate a 5MB in-memory PDF-like binary blob with valid PDF header
POST the file to /documents/upload with Content-Type multipart/form-data
Capture the HTTP response status and JSON body
expected: Response JSON contains 'stored' equal to true
trace: R-WEB-077: valid PDF <=10MB must be stored and virus scan queued
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io

def test_upload_valid_pdf_under_limit_stores_document_and_queues_scan(
    auth_session,       # <fixture>: requests.Session with auth headers
    upload_url,         # <fixture>: str base URL to /documents/upload
    make_file_payload,  # <helper_fn>: (size_bytes, mime, ext) -> (filename, BytesIO, mime)
):
    filename, data, mime = make_file_payload(5 * 1024 * 1024, "application/pdf", "pdf")
    resp = auth_session.post(
        upload_url,
        files={"file": (filename, data, mime)},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "document_id" in body
    assert body.get("virus_scan_status") == "queued"
    assert body.get("stored") is True

tests.name: test_upload_file_exceeding_10mb_returns_413
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
Target endpoint /documents/upload is reachable
A PDF file strictly greater than 10MB is prepared
actions: Generate an in-memory binary blob of 10MB + 1 byte with PDF header
POST the file to /documents/upload with Content-Type multipart/form-data
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 413 Request Entity Too Large is returned
Response body contains an error message indicating file size limit exceeded
tests.executable:
def test_upload_file_exceeding_10mb_returns_413(
    auth_session,
    upload_url,
    make_file_payload,
):
    oversized = 10 * 1024 * 1024 + 1  # 10MB + 1 byte
    filename, data, mime = make_file_payload(oversized, "application/pdf", "pdf")
    resp = auth_session.post(
        upload_url,
        files={"file": (filename, data, mime)},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 413
    body = resp.text.lower()
    assert any(kw in body for kw in ("size", "too large", "limit", "413"))

tests.name: test_upload_disallowed_mime_type_returns_415
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
Target endpoint /documents/upload is reachable
A file with an unsupported MIME type (text/csv) under 10MB is prepared
actions: Generate a 1MB in-memory binary blob with Content-Type text/csv and .csv extension
POST the file to /documents/upload with Content-Type multipart/form-data
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 415 Unsupported Media Type is returned
Response body contains an error message indicating unsupported file type
trace: R-WEB-077: error pathway - 415 when mime not in allow-list
tests.executable:
def test_upload_disallowed_mime_type_returns_415(
    auth_session,
    upload_url,
    make_file_payload,
):
    filename, data, mime = make_file_payload(1 * 1024 * 1024, "text/csv", "csv")
    resp = auth_session.post(
        upload_url,
        files={"file": (filename, data, mime)},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 415
    body = resp.text.lower()
    assert any(kw in body for kw in ("unsupported", "mime", "type", "format", "415"))

tests.name: test_upload_file_at_exact_10mb_boundary_is_accepted
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
Target endpoint /documents/upload is reachable
A PNG file of exactly 10485760 bytes (10 * 1024 * 1024) is prepared
actions: Generate an in-memory binary blob of exactly 10485760 bytes with image/png MIME
POST the file to /documents/upload with Content-Type multipart/form-data
Capture the HTTP response status and JSON body
expected: HTTP 201 Created is returned (boundary value must be accepted)
trace: R-WEB-077: BVA upper boundary - exactly 10MB must be accepted, not rejected
tests.executable:
def test_upload_file_at_exact_10mb_boundary_is_accepted(
    auth_session,
    upload_url,
    make_file_payload,
):
    exact_limit = 10 * 1024 * 1024  # 10485760 bytes
    filename, data, mime = make_file_payload(exact_limit, "image/png", "png")
    resp = auth_session.post(
        upload_url,
        files={"file": (filename, data, mime)},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, (
        f"Expected 201 at exact 10MB boundary, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "document_id" in body
    assert body.get("virus_scan_status") == "queued"

tests.name: test_upload_file_at_10mb_plus_one_byte_is_rejected
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
Target endpoint /documents/upload is reachable
A JPG file of exactly 10485761 bytes (10MB + 1 byte) is prepared
actions: Generate an in-memory binary blob of 10485761 bytes with image/jpeg MIME
POST the file to /documents/upload with Content-Type multipart/form-data
Capture the HTTP response status
expected: HTTP 413 is returned (one byte over the boundary must be rejected)
Response body references the size limit
trace: R-WEB-077: BVA upper boundary+1 - 10MB+1 byte must yield HTTP 413
tests.executable:
def test_upload_file_at_10mb_plus_one_byte_is_rejected(
    auth_session,
    upload_url,
    make_file_payload,
):
    over_limit = 10 * 1024 * 1024 + 1  # 10485761 bytes
    filename, data, mime = make_file_payload(over_limit, "image/jpeg", "jpg")
    resp = auth_session.post(
        upload_url,
        files={"file": (filename, data, mime)},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 413, (
        f"Expected 413 at 10MB+1 byte, got {resp.status_code}"
    )

tests.name: test_upload_transitions_document_state_to_stored_then_scan_queued
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
Target endpoint /documents/upload is reachable
GET /documents/{id} endpoint is available to query document state
A valid JPG file under 10MB is available
actions: POST a 1MB JPG file to /documents/upload
Assert initial response indicates document_id and virus_scan_status=queued
GET /documents/{document_id} to verify persisted state reflects stored=true
expected: POST returns HTTP 201 with document_id and virus_scan_status queued
Subsequent GET returns HTTP 200 with stored=true and virus_scan_status in (queued, scanning, clean)
Document state has transitioned from non-existent to stored
trace: R-WEB-077: expected outcome - document stored; virus scan queued; state transition coverage
tests.executable:
def test_upload_transitions_document_state_to_stored_then_scan_queued(
    auth_session,
    upload_url,
    documents_url,      # <fixture>: str base URL for /documents
    make_file_payload,
):
    filename, data, mime = make_file_payload(1 * 1024 * 1024, "image/jpeg", "jpg")
    post_resp = auth_session.post(
        upload_url,
        files={"file": (filename, data, mime)},
    )
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201
    body = post_resp.json()
    doc_id = body["document_id"]
    assert body.get("virus_scan_status") == "queued"

    get_resp = auth_session.get(f"{documents_url}/{doc_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    doc = get_resp.json()
    assert doc.get("stored") is True
    assert doc.get("virus_scan_status") in ("queued", "scanning", "clean")

tests.name: test_upload_all_allowed_mime_types_are_accepted
preconditions: User is authenticated with a valid session token
Target endpoint /documents/upload is reachable
Small representative files for PDF, PNG, and JPG MIME types are available
actions: For each of (application/pdf/.pdf), (image/png/.png), (image/jpeg/.jpg): POST a 512KB file
Capture HTTP status code for each upload
expected: Each response contains a unique document_id
trace: R-WEB-077: all three allowed MIME types must be accepted (equivalence class: valid mime)
tests.executable:
import pytest

@pytest.mark.parametrize("mime,ext", [
    ("application/pdf", "pdf"),
    ("image/png",       "png"),
    ("image/jpeg",      "jpg"),
])
def test_upload_all_allowed_mime_types_are_accepted(
    auth_session,
    upload_url,
    make_file_payload,
    mime,
    ext,
):
    filename, data, content_type = make_file_payload(512 * 1024, mime, ext)
    resp = auth_session.post(
        upload_url,
        files={"file": (filename, data, content_type)},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, (
        f"MIME {mime} should be accepted, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    assert "document_id" in resp.json()
